skill.name: wan-2-7
skill.description: Generate short Wan 2.7 text-to-video clips through the RunComfy CLI, including lip-sync, prompt expansion, aspect ratio, resolution, duration, and output guidance.
skill.summary: Create Wan 2.7 text-to-video clips on RunComfy with documented inputs and CLI invocation guidance.
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but most are false positives from Markdown backticks, endpoint documentation, and ordinary prose. The confirmed risk is that the skill intentionally invokes the RunComfy CLI, sends prompts and optional media URLs to RunComfy over HTTPS, and documents local token storage, so publication should include a network and credential-handling warning.

medium_findings.title: External Model API Invocation
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to run the RunComfy CLI for Wan 2.7 video generation. This is intended behavior, but it sends user prompts and optional audio or media URLs to an external RunComfy model service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The CLI command and outbound RunComfy endpoint are explicitly documented. The behavior appears legitimate for this skill, but it is a real third-party data transfer risk.

medium_findings.title: Credential Storage and Environment Token Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill documents RunComfy authentication through browser login or RUNCOMFY_TOKEN. It also states that login writes an API token to a hidden config path in the user home directory.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token location and environment-variable option are directly described in the skill. This is normal CLI credential handling, but users must protect the token.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Alerts Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged many backtick and shell-command patterns. Review found these are Markdown inline code, tables, and fenced examples for the documented RunComfy workflow, not embedded executable Ruby or hidden script code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown and contains no Ruby source or executable helper files. The flagged lines are documentation examples or inline command names.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported weak cryptographic algorithm blockers, but the cited lines contain frontmatter prose, anti-pattern text, and an exit-codes heading. No cryptographic operation or hashing algorithm use was found in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations do not contain crypto code or algorithm selection. The signal appears to come from substring matching in ordinary Markdown text.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Alert Is Placeholder URL Text
low_findings.description: The path traversal finding points to an example audio URL placeholder. It does not instruct filesystem traversal and is not used as a local path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 78 is an HTTPS audio_url example with ellipsis text. There is no file-read operation or traversal sequence applied to the local filesystem.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alert Is Model Routing Guidance
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance finding points to a sentence telling the assistant when to route requests to this model. It does not collect host, user, process, or network information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is plain routing guidance inside the skill instructions. No reconnaissance command or environment inspection is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: CLI Execution With External Network Effects
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented command runs a local CLI that submits jobs to RunComfy and downloads generated output. Users should expect outbound network calls and generated files in the requested output directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command and network behavior are stated in the skill. This is expected functionality, but it is still a sensitive execution pattern for marketplace review.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hidden Credential File Reference
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill references a hidden token file under the user home config directory. This is normal for CLI authentication but requires careful permissions and secret handling.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact token path and 0600 permission claim are documented. No evidence shows malicious access, but it is a meaningful credential-handling pattern.

content.value_statement: Teams need repeatable prompts and settings for short AI video generation. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Wan 2.7 requests using the RunComfy CLI.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use Wan 2.7 instead of related video models.
Documents supported Wan 2.7 inputs, including prompt, audio_url, duration, resolution, aspect ratio, seed, and negative_prompt.
Provides RunComfy CLI command patterns for default, lip-sync, and literal-control video generation.
Gives prompt-writing guidance for camera motion, action scope, prompt expansion, and negative prompts.
Summarizes known limits for duration, resolution, audio format, aspect ratios, and reference media.
limitations: Requires the RunComfy CLI and a RunComfy account or token.
Sends prompts and optional media URLs to RunComfy over the network.
Wan 2.7 output is limited to short clips with a 1080p ceiling.
The skill documents usage; it does not include its own video model or renderer.

use_cases.title: Produce lip-synced ads
use_cases.description: Create short spokesperson clips from a written prompt and a hosted voiceover file.

use_cases.title: Prototype product videos
use_cases.description: Generate quick product showcase clips with controlled camera movement and aspect ratio.

use_cases.title: Prepare model API jobs
use_cases.description: Turn creative requirements into RunComfy CLI inputs that match the Wan 2.7 schema.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic clip
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Wan 2.7 on RunComfy to generate a 5 second 1080p 16:9 video of [subject]. Include a clear camera move and one primary action.

prompt_templates.title: Make a vertical social video
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 9:16 Wan 2.7 video prompt for [product or scene]. Keep it under 10 seconds and include motion, lighting, and a negative prompt.

prompt_templates.title: Generate lip-sync video
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Wan 2.7 with this audio_url: [URL]. Build a prompt for a medium close-up spokesperson video, set duration to match the audio, and choose the best aspect ratio.

prompt_templates.title: Plan controlled variants
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare three Wan 2.7 RunComfy variants for [campaign]. Keep the same seed for consistency, change one creative variable per variant, and disable prompt expansion where literal control matters.

output_examples.input: Request for a product showcase clip
output_examples.output: A concise Wan 2.7 prompt with a slow camera push-in, 1080p resolution, 16:9 aspect ratio, and a targeted negative prompt.

output_examples.input: Request for a voiceover-led social ad
output_examples.output: A lip-sync setup that uses the supplied audio URL, matches duration to the audio, and selects 9:16 for vertical delivery.
A literal-control setup that disables prompt expansion and keeps the creative direction focused on one action.

best_practices: Use one primary action per clip so motion remains clear.
Host audio files securely and verify they match Wan 2.7 size, duration, and format limits.
Reuse a seed for close variants and change the seed for broader exploration.
anti_patterns: Do not send secrets, private recordings, or unreleased campaign assets unless RunComfy use is approved.
Do not combine many unrelated actions in one short clip prompt.
Do not use vague negative prompts when specific visual issues should be avoided.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It guides an assistant through generating Wan 2.7 text-to-video jobs with the RunComfy CLI.

faq.question: Does it require external network access?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented workflow submits model jobs to RunComfy and downloads generated outputs.

faq.question: Can it create lip-synced video?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents the audio_url input for audio-driven lip-sync when the audio file meets Wan 2.7 limits.

faq.question: What tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What are the main output limits?
faq.answer: Wan 2.7 is documented with short duration limits, supported aspect ratios, and a 1080p maximum resolution.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a warning because it intentionally uses a third-party model API and handles RunComfy credentials.
